entry.title: GenAI Signal — 2026-07-18
entry.content:
I still believe that everyone is too fixated on the state of play in AI right now (which labs are ahead, how to manage c

Ethan Mollick · 11:23 PM PT

Ethan Mollick argues the field overemphasizes current lab rankings and costs while underestimating the steep near-term capability curve.

Why it matters: Meta-analysis of field attention; signals major capability gains expected near-term, reshapes strategic focus.

AI Mania Is Eviscerating Global Decision-Making

Simon Willison · 10:06 PM PT

Critical analysis of AI hype corrupting corporate decision-making: executives pursue AI initiatives without hands-on experience, distorting business strategy.

Why it matters: Substantive assessment of AI's organizational risk; exposes strategy-destructive effects of unjustified hype.

I think what is pretty clear is that the Chinese labs are far more capital efficient. In a world where scaling labs are

Nathan Lambert · 3:34 PM PT

Nathan Lambert argues Chinese labs achieve superior capital efficiency; in a compute-driven race, efficiency may be the decisive edge.

Why it matters: Economic assessment of competitive advantage; efficiency could dominate raw spending in AI outcomes.

We need an operation warp speed for state capacity and other independent evaluation (and understanding) of frontier mode

Nathan Lambert · 9:29 AM PT

Nathan Lambert calls for 'operation warp speed' on state capacity and independent evaluation of frontier models.

Why it matters: Policy proposal to improve AI governance infrastructure; addresses critical gap in frontier model oversight.

When I asked Kimi K3 "I want you to suggest two poems that you think apply to the current state of GenAI models like you

Ethan Mollick · 10:51 PM PT

Kimi K3 generated 32-page chain-of-thought when asked for non-obvious poems applying to GenAI, exhibiting characteristic looping and dead-ends.

Why it matters: Substantive observation of frontier model's reasoning patterns and failure modes.

Interestingly, when I made a request in Chinese for Kimi K3 to pick two non-cliched poems that apply to LLMs, 95.5% of t

Ethan Mollick · 4:24 PM PT

Kimi K3 prompted in Chinese to select non-cliched poems for LLMs produces 32-page reasoning that is 95.5% English characters, despite Chinese prompt and context.

Why it matters: Technical finding on model behavior; shows unexpected language dominance in reasoning chains.

entry.title: GenAI Signal — 2026-07-19
entry.content:
Demis Hassabis on the New Coming Age

Zvi Mowshowitz · 7:11 AM PT

Demis Hassabis published framework essay 'A Framework for Frontier AI and the Dawning of a New Age'; Zvi analyzes and responds.

Why it matters: Frontier lab leader's strategic vision on AI development trajectory; core signal for understanding frontier AI direction.

If recent events with Kimi K3 have finally convinced you that you need to try and understand how the Chinese labs approa

Nathan Lambert · 7:23 AM PT

Nathan Lambert recommends understanding how Chinese labs approach AI differently from SF-based labs.

Why it matters: Geopolitical analysis of alternative AI paradigms; critical for mapping global competitive dynamics.

London Emerges as Global Hub for AI Safety Testing - 조선일보

gnews_google_deepmind · 1:03 AM PT

London emerging as major hub for AI safety testing and evaluation infrastructure.

Why it matters: Policy development in global AI governance; distributes frontier model evaluation outside US.

AI Safety Grades Are In: No Lab Tops C+, and the Best Ones Are Retreating - Tech Times

gnews_google_deepmind · 8:25 AM PT

AI safety grading system shows no lab exceeds C+; top performers are retreating on safety commitments.

Why it matters: Substantive safety assessment of frontier labs; indicates systematic gaps in safety implementation.
